replies.text:
@nathanielbass771 ...Wait. It sounds like we're talking about separate indictments. The one I was looking at was the most recent I know of, occuring around the end of 2025 with an appeal filed at the start of February this year. There was only one property being referred to, and the initial charges pointed to a number of actual discrepancies - in parts of documents which were nonrelevant to their purpose (we're talking boilerplate forms used for a number of reasons) and so which got by because no party including auditors had any reason to look at them: she did not benefit from this happening and unfortunately discrepancies like this aren't uncommon for paperwork-heavy fields like real estate where attorneys use multipurpose boilerplate forms because it's also not worth the expense of drafting custom documents each time. As a result, nobody normally prosecutes as you can't find harm or intent: This is why the FBI said they couldn't find anything there to indict her over, why federal prosecutors in Virginia said they didn't have anything to indict her over, and the federal attorney for the eastern district of Virginia was then replaced by one of Trump's personal attorneys who honestly isn't qualified for the position.

replies.author: @Lorkanthal
replies.text:
@captainrev4959 you understand that the city evaluates your property for tax purposes right?  Every single piece of private property is worth more in the private market then it is to the government for tax purposes.  This includes everything from residential houses to large commercial buildings.

Also, due to statute of limitations she had to upgrade it to a felony by saying he committed this "fraud" to conceal some other crime.  what was that other crime?  No one knows since it was never specified.

replies.author: @captainrev4959
replies.text:
@Lor@Lorkanthalk, maybe that’s how it works for your house, but when the government is determining tax on a massive property like some of the ones trump owns, they usually have to get statements from accountants and the owner to properly asses the property. And the difference was not something that could be made up for by the private market. 

He was claiming that Mar-a-Lago was worth more than 140 million dollars on loan declarations, despite the fact that the property has a contractual obligation that prevents it from being developed into anything. Trump entered into that contract himself to pay lower Florida state taxes, and now the property is stuck as a resort. Even if he sells it that doesn’t go away, so no one is going to buy it for a large mark up since they can’t do anything with it. The highest that you could sell a piece of property of that size for is 40 or 50 million. that is a massive difference from what he put down on loan asset declarations.

There was also his penthouse apartment in New York City, he claimed it to be more than 10,000 ft squared on his loan declarations, it’s actually 7000. And to put that difference in perspective, if it were 10,000 ft.² that would make Trump tower the second most valuable piece of private property in all of New York City. At 7000 it’s not even top 10 in terms of value. That is another massive lie. You wanna know what he put down on his taxes for the size? He put down 7000.

There are more of these I could go into, one of them he valued some apartments at 50 million despite similar apartments in NYC selling for 750k. that’s 66 times the value. But I don’t care enough to list everything. 

As for the “other crime”, you seem to be thinking of a rico charge, that is usually the only time that you can bypass the statute of limitations like that, and that’s not what he was charged with. Trump was charged under New York executive law 63(12), which has no such statute extension.(you may be thinking of diddy, he was charged with rico using that method in New York) Anyway the reason Trump was charged with this despite the six year statute of limitations is because the original complaint was filed in 2020, meaning acts that happened in 2015 were able to be used. It wasn’t publicly announced until later and didn’t move on to court until 2022 and 23 because of discovery and various appeal delays. 

The point being he really did commit that crime, and the argument that the banks were ok with it isn’t a defense. And is also hilarious because I’ve never heard of a bank that is ok with being lied to so someone can pay less interest. I guarantee you the banks would’ve preferred if he told the truth so they could charge him more money for the loan.
